purpose.summary: This COBOL program interacts with an IMS database to retrieve and update pending authorization details. It retrieves authorization summary and detail segments based on account ID and authorization key, and allows marking authorizations as fraudulent.
purpose.business_context: This program likely supports a fraud detection or authorization management process within a financial institution or similar organization.

inputs.name: PAUTSUM0
inputs.description: Pending Authorization Summary segment, containing account ID and other summary information.

inputs.name: PAUTDTL1
inputs.description: Pending Authorization Details segment, containing authorization key and other detailed information about the authorization.

inputs.name: WS-ACCT-ID
inputs.description: Account ID passed to the program, used to retrieve authorization data.

inputs.name: WS-AUTH-KEY
inputs.description: Authorization key passed to the program, used to retrieve authorization details.

outputs.name: PENDING-AUTH-DETAILS
outputs.description: Updated Pending Authorization Details segment, with fraud indicators set.

outputs.name: WS-MESSAGE
outputs.description: Message displayed on the CICS screen, indicating success or failure of the operation.

called_programs.purpose: Displays the authorization view screen with relevant data and messages.

business_rules.description: If the PSB is scheduled more than once, terminate the PSB and reschedule it.
business_rules.logic_summary: Checks if PSB-SCHEDULED-MORE-THAN-ONCE is true, and if so, performs DLI TERM and SCHD.

business_rules.description: If the IMS call to update the authorization details is successful, take a syncpoint. Otherwise, rollback the transaction.
business_rules.logic_summary: Evaluates the DIBSTAT after the REPL call. If STATUS-OK, performs TAKE-SYNCPOINT; otherwise, performs ROLL-BACK.

copybooks_used.purpose: Defines the structure of the PAUTSUM0 IMS segment.
Defines the structure of the PAUTDTL1 IMS segment.

paragraphs.purpose: This paragraph retrieves authorization records from the IMS database. It first calls SCHEDULE-PSB to schedule the PSB. It then moves the account ID and authorization key from working storage to the PAUTSUM0 segment fields. It performs a GU (Get Unique) call to retrieve the PAUTSUM0 segment based on the account ID. If the segment is found, it sets AUTHS-NOT-EOF to TRUE. If not found or at the end of the database, it sets AUTHS-EOF to TRUE. If any other error occurs, it sets WS-ERR-FLG to 'Y', constructs an error message, and calls SEND-AUTHVIEW-SCREEN to display the error. If the PAUTSUM0 segment is successfully retrieved, it performs a GNP (Get Next within Parent) call to retrieve the PAUTDTL1 segment based on the authorization key. Similar error handling is performed for the GNP call. The paragraph consumes WS-ACCT-ID and WS-AUTH-KEY as input, and outputs data to PENDING-AUTH-SUMMARY and PENDING-AUTH-DETAILS. It calls SCHEDULE-PSB and SEND-AUTHVIEW-SCREEN.

This paragraph retrieves the next authorization detail record from the IMS database. It performs a GNP (Get Next within Parent) call to retrieve the PAUTDTL1 segment. If the segment is found, it sets AUTHS-NOT-EOF to TRUE. If not found or at the end of the database, it sets AUTHS-EOF to TRUE. If any other error occurs, it sets WS-ERR-FLG to 'Y', constructs an error message, and calls SEND-AUTHVIEW-SCREEN to display the error. This paragraph is used to iterate through the authorization details for a given account. It consumes no explicit input parameters but relies on the current position within the IMS database. It outputs data to PENDING-AUTH-DETAILS. It calls SEND-AUTHVIEW-SCREEN.

This paragraph updates the authorization details in the IMS database. It moves the fraud authorization record from WS-FRAUD-AUTH-RECORD to the PENDING-AUTH-DETAILS segment. It then performs a REPL (Replace) call to update the PAUTDTL1 segment in the IMS database. If the update is successful (STATUS-OK), it performs TAKE-SYNCPOINT to commit the changes. It then moves a success message to WS-MESSAGE, indicating whether the authorization was marked or removed as fraudulent. If the update fails, it performs ROLL-BACK to undo any changes. It sets WS-ERR-FLG to 'Y', constructs an error message, and calls SEND-AUTHVIEW-SCREEN to display the error. This paragraph consumes WS-FRAUD-AUTH-RECORD as input and updates the PENDING-AUTH-DETAILS segment. It calls TAKE-SYNCPOINT, ROLL-BACK, and SEND-AUTHVIEW-SCREEN.

This paragraph takes a CICS syncpoint to commit the changes made to the IMS database. It executes a CICS SYNCPOINT command. If the syncpoint is successful, the changes are committed. If it fails, the transaction may be left in an inconsistent state. This paragraph does not consume any specific input and does not produce any specific output other than committing the transaction. It does not call any other paragraphs or programs.

This paragraph rolls back the CICS transaction to undo any changes made to the IMS database. It executes a CICS SYNCPOINT ROLLBACK command. This is typically called when an error occurs during the update process. This paragraph does not consume any specific input and does not produce any specific output other than rolling back the transaction. It does not call any other paragraphs or programs.

This paragraph schedules the PSB (Program Specification Block) for IMS database access. It executes a DLI SCHD command with the PSB name. If the PSB has been scheduled more than once (PSB-SCHEDULED-MORE-THAN-ONCE), it terminates the PSB and then schedules it again. If the scheduling is successful (STATUS-OK), it sets IMS-PSB-SCHD to TRUE. If the scheduling fails, it sets WS-ERR-FLG to 'Y', constructs an error message, and calls SEND-AUTHVIEW-SCREEN to display the error. This paragraph consumes the PSB-NAME as input and sets the IMS-PSB-SCHD indicator. It calls SEND-AUTHVIEW-SCREEN.

error_handling.condition: DIBSTAT not equal to STATUS-OK, SEGMENT-NOT-FOUND, or END-OF-DB during READ-AUTH-RECORD
error_handling.action: Set WS-ERR-FLG to 'Y', construct an error message, and call SEND-AUTHVIEW-SCREEN.

error_handling.condition: DIBSTAT not equal to STATUS-OK, SEGMENT-NOT-FOUND, or END-OF-DB during READ-NEXT-AUTH-RECORD
error_handling.action: Set WS-ERR-FLG to 'Y', construct an error message, and call SEND-AUTHVIEW-SCREEN.

error_handling.condition: DIBSTAT not equal to STATUS-OK during UPDATE-AUTH-DETAILS
error_handling.action: Perform ROLL-BACK, set WS-ERR-FLG to 'Y', construct an error message, and call SEND-AUTHVIEW-SCREEN.

error_handling.condition: DIBSTAT not equal to STATUS-OK during SCHEDULE-PSB
error_handling.action: Set WS-ERR-FLG to 'Y', construct an error message, and call SEND-AUTHVIEW-SCREEN.

cics_operations.purpose: Rolls back changes to the database.
